reason: Both answers are nearly identical in structure and content: alert audit, page-only-on-symptoms/actionable rule, weekly review/budget mechanism, and both correctly conclude six-person weekly rotation is fine and shouldn't be restructured. Neither mentions reducing sprint commitment during the on-call week, a shared gap. B adds routing non-urgent alerts to business hours and folding hygiene into handoff; A adds a closing reasoning frame distinguishing reliability vs. monitoring problems. These minor differences roughly offset, and neither contains a factual error.
